subject: Public Health
title: Leprosy in the Medieval World
description: A quick, practical guide to Leprosy in the Medieval World.

hook.text: Lepers were cast out of medieval society, forced to ring bells to warn others of their approach.

buildup.text: Leprosy—caused by Mycobacterium leprae—damages nerves and skin, disfiguring slowly over years.

discovery.text: Medieval Europe built over 19,000 leprosariums—quarantine colonies for those deemed unclean.

twist.text: Leprosy is actually one of the least contagious diseases—95% of people are naturally immune.

climax.text: Stigma outlasted the disease. Even today, the word 'leper' carries shame despite effective treatment.

punchline.text: Fear of disease can be more contagious than disease itself.

quiz.question: What percentage of people are naturally immune to leprosy?
